iti41:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti41:2.1: “Monks, those beings are truly deprived who are deprived of noble discernment.
iti41:2.2: They live in stress in the present life–troubled, distressed, & feverish–
iti41:2.3: and at the break-up of the body, after death, a bad destination can be expected.
iti41:2.4: “Those beings are not deprived who are not deprived of noble discernment.
iti41:2.5: They live in ease in the present life–untroubled, undistressed, & not feverish–
iti41:2.6: and at the break-up of the body, after death, a good destination can be expected.
iti41:3.2:
Look at the world –including its heavenly beings:
deprived of discernment,
iti41:3.4: it conceives that ‘This is the truth.’
iti41:4.1: The best discernment in the world
iti41:4.4: the total ending of birth & becoming.
iti41:5.1: Human & heavenly beings hold them dear:
iti41:5.2: those who are self-awakened, mindful, bearing their last bodies
